title: DNB Hub
description: Full-stack MERN platform to manage Drum & Bass events, artists, and promoters. Includes JWT authentication, protected routes, and CRUD dashboards powered by a structured REST API with clear separation between frontend and backend, using MongoDB and Mongoose relationships.
shortDescription: Full-stack MERN app with JWT auth, protected routes, and CRUD dashboards for events, artists, and promoters.
keyFeatures: JWT authentication and protected routes
REST API with MongoDB/Mongoose models and relationships
Full CRUD dashboards for events, artists, and promoters
learningHighlights: Designed data models and relationships in MongoDB with Mongoose
Implemented authentication flows (signup/login/verify) with JWT
Built and deployed a full-stack app end-to-end with clean separation of concerns

title: InspectoRepo
description: Local web application built to review TypeScript and React codebases through deterministic AST-based analysis. It scans selected folders, detects maintainability issues, scores the codebase, and generates structured Markdown reports in a VSCode-like interface.
shortDescription: Local TypeScript/React code review tool that analyzes TS/TSX projects with AST-based rules and exports structured Markdown reports.
keyFeatures: Deterministic TS/TSX analysis pipeline using AST rules
Folder selection and project scanning with local-first workflow
Filterable issue list with detailed suggestions and proposed patches
Markdown report export for shareable code review output
Monorepo structure with separated web, core, and shared packages
learningHighlights: Built a local-first developer tool instead of a standard CRUD app
Worked with TypeScript AST analysis using ts-morph
Structured a monorepo architecture with reusable packages
Focused on deterministic analysis and practical report generation
Strengthened experience with tool-building and developer UX

title: Website Maker Pro
description: Interactive web tool for generating business website demos from a configurable form. It supports multiple business categories, reusable landing-page sections, style variations, local saves, shareable URLs, and briefing export, making it useful for quickly presenting tailored website concepts to different clients.
shortDescription: Website demo generator that creates tailored business landing pages with real-time preview, reusable sections, and shareable configurations.
keyFeatures: Real-time website preview driven by a configurable business form
Multiple business categories with reusable section-based templates
Style variants and content customization for tailored website demos
Shareable demo URLs using query parameters
Local save/load flow and briefing export for presentation-ready handoff
learningHighlights: Built a configuration-driven UI instead of a standard CRUD application
Structured reusable page sections for multiple business scenarios
Worked with URL state, localStorage persistence, and export flows
Strengthened TypeScript component architecture in a template-based project
Improved UX thinking around preview, customization, and client presentation workflows

title: FoundIt
description: A second-hand marketplace built with React. Includes client-side search, multi-filter + sorting, pagination, favorites via localStorage, and full CRUD connected to a mock REST API (json-server).
shortDescription: React marketplace application with search, filters, sorting, pagination, favorites, and CRUD via REST API.
keyFeatures: Search, multi-filtering and sorting
Pagination with consistent UX on filter changes
Favorites stored in localStorage
Full CRUD (create/edit/delete) via REST API
Product status handling (available/sold)

title: Dancefloor Defender
description: Retro arcade shooter built with vanilla JavaScript. Defend the dancefloor through progressive difficulty, multiple enemy behaviors, power-ups, and a persistent Top 10 leaderboard using localStorage.
shortDescription: Vanilla JavaScript arcade shooter with progressive difficulty, mobile controls, and persistent high scores.
keyFeatures: Progressive difficulty and level ramp
Multiple enemy types and distinct behaviors
Power-ups (risk/reward mechanic)
Persistent leaderboard and settings via localStorage
Touch-friendly controls for mobile

title: Chess
description: Interactive chess application built from scratch in TypeScript, combining a framework-agnostic chess engine with a React UI. The project handles full legal move generation, special rules, notation, FEN import/export, undo, and endgame state detection.
shortDescription: TypeScript chess engine and React interface built from scratch with legal move generation, notation, FEN support, and undo.
keyFeatures: Full legal move generation with check, pin, castling, en passant, and promotion
Checkmate, stalemate, draw detection, and move validation
Standard Algebraic Notation (SAN) support with disambiguation
FEN import/export with validation
Undo support with correct restoration of game state and move history panel
learningHighlights: Built core game logic from scratch instead of relying on a chess library
Practiced state modelling for complex rule-driven behaviour
Kept the engine framework-agnostic and separated from the UI
Improved reasoning around edge cases and move validation
Strengthened TypeScript problem-solving in a logic-heavy project

title: FunMachine Synth
description: FM synthesizer created in Max MSP as a custom sound design instrument. The project focuses on frequency modulation synthesis, giving control over core synthesis parameters to generate a wide range of tones, textures, and timbral variations.
shortDescription: FM synthesizer built in Max MSP to explore sound synthesis, modulation, and custom instrument design.
keyFeatures: Custom FM synthesis architecture built in Max MSP
Real-time control of modulation and timbre-shaping parameters
Wide sonic range from clean tones to complex textured sounds
Instrument-oriented interface for live experimentation and sound design
learningHighlights: Built a synthesis tool from scratch instead of using a prebuilt instrument
Deepened understanding of FM synthesis and modulation behaviour
Improved audio programming logic through parameter-driven sound generation
Strengthened experience in designing interactive digital instruments

title: Sound(E)scapes
description: Interactive sound installation developed in Max MSP to create an immersive listening experience through spatial movement. Four different real-world soundscapes are triggered when someone enters specific areas, while movement between zones blends those environments with added effects, transforming them into hybrid and unreal soundscapes.
shortDescription: Interactive Max MSP sound installation that blends real-world soundscapes based on the listener’s position.
keyFeatures: Position-based triggering of four independent soundscapes
Dynamic blending between zones for hybrid sound transitions
Effect processing to transform realistic ambiences into unreal textures
Immersive installation-style interaction driven by physical movement
learningHighlights: Designed an interactive audio system based on spatial behaviour
Worked with layered sound environments and real-time transitions
Explored immersive sound design beyond traditional linear playback
Strengthened creative coding skills in Max MSP for interactive installations

title: Step-Sequencer
description: Experimental melodic and rhythmic step sequencer developed in Max MSP and controlled through camera input. The system tracks physical objects placed in view of the camera and uses that interaction to drive sequencing behaviour, turning a simple physical setup into a playful audio-control interface.
shortDescription: Camera-controlled melodic and rhythmic step sequencer built in Max MSP using physical object interaction.
keyFeatures: Melodic and rhythmic sequencing controlled through camera input
Physical object interaction used as a playful control surface
Real-time mapping between movement and musical output
Combination of sequencing logic with experimental interface design
learningHighlights: Connected visual input with musical sequencing in real time
Explored alternative human-computer interaction through physical objects
Built rule-based sequencing behaviour inside Max MSP
Strengthened problem-solving across sound, interaction, and system design

title: Bounce (p5.js)
description: Browser-based recreation of my original Java/Processing arcade game, converted to p5.js for the web. The project keeps the core collision-based gameplay while adapting the original visual and audio assets to a browser-friendly version, with score and lives progression, increasing speed, and an additional missile hazard unlocked during longer runs.
shortDescription: p5.js web version of my original Java/Processing arcade game, with collision-based gameplay, score progression, and adapted assets.
keyFeatures: 2D arcade gameplay recreated for the browser with p5.js
Score and lives system with progressive speed scaling
Missile hazard introduced after reaching a score threshold
Music and sound effect toggles through keyboard and HUD controls
Original visual and audio assets adapted from the earlier Processing version
learningHighlights: Converted an earlier Java/Processing game into a browser-based p5.js project
Adapted game logic, controls, and assets to a lightweight web setup
Practiced collision handling, progression balancing, and browser-based audio integration
Strengthened experience in translating an existing project into a web-friendly version

title: Audio Reactive Visualizer
description: Browser-based p5.js adaptation of my original Java/Processing audio-reactive visualizer. The project combines reactive generative visuals with interactive playback controls, custom track loading, loop-region editing, and browser-friendly audio analysis to turn a Processing sketch into a more accessible web experience.
shortDescription: p5.js web adaptation of my original Java/Processing audio-reactive visualizer, with interactive playback controls and loopable reactive visuals.
keyFeatures: Audio-reactive visuals driven by waveform, amplitude, and peak changes
Interactive player with play/pause, restart, volume, and seek controls
Support for bundled demo audio and user-selected local tracks
Loop mode with draggable start and end markers
Fullscreen browser canvas optimized for lightweight deployment
learningHighlights: Adapted an earlier Java/Processing project into a browser-based p5.js experience
Worked with audio analysis, waveform rendering, and reactive visual behaviour
Designed a more complete playback UI instead of a simple visual sketch
Strengthened experience in translating creative coding work into a deployable web format
